_comment: MANIFEST V3: Chrome's current extension platform (V2 was sunset in 2024).
V3 replaced persistent background pages with short-lived service workers,
tightened permissions, and banned remote code execution for security.
WHY EACH PERMISSION:
tabCapture  — captures the audio stream of a specific browser tab (the meeting tab).
Unlike the 'microphone' permission, tabCapture captures the actual
meeting call audio that is already decoded and playing in the tab,
giving us clean speech without room echo or competing microphone noise.
activeTab   — grants temporary access to the currently focused tab when the user
clicks the extension icon, without requiring broad host permissions.
storage     — persists the active meeting ID and user settings (API URL, device index)
in Chrome's local storage so they survive popup close/reopen.
scripting   — allows the background service worker to programmatically inject
content.js into meeting tabs at runtime.
WHY tabCapture INSTEAD OF MICROPHONE:
The 'microphone' permission records from a physical input device (your mic).
In a remote meeting the other participants' voices arrive as decoded audio
playing through your speakers — not through your mic. tabCapture intercepts
the decoded audio stream directly inside the browser tab before it reaches
the speakers, giving us all participants' speech with no hardware setup.
ICON NOTE:
Icons will be added before the final demo.
name: MeetMind — Meeting Intelligence
description: AI-powered live transcription and action item detection for meetings
